description: Schematic figure: ordered RNAP assembly pathway, conserved catalytic aspartate motif, and conserved-core/variable-periphery concept for the review
code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
from matplotlib.patches import FancyArrowPatch, FancyBboxPatch
import numpy as np

fig = plt.figure(figsize=(13, 9))

# ---------- Panel A: ordered assembly pathway ----------
axA = fig.add_axes([0.05, 0.55, 0.9, 0.4]); axA.axis('off')
axA.set_xlim(0,10); axA.set_ylim(0,3)
axA.set_title("A  Ordered assembly of the bacterial RNAP core enzyme (Ishihama 1981; Kimura & Ishihama 1996)",
              fontsize=12, loc='left', weight='bold')

stages = [
    ("2$\\alpha$", "monomers", 0.7),
    (r"$\alpha_2$", "dimer platform", 2.4),
    (r"$\alpha_2\beta$", "+ $\\beta$", 4.1),
    (r"$\alpha_2\beta\beta'$", "premature core\n(inactive)", 5.9),
    (r"$\alpha_2\beta\beta'\omega$", "CORE (E)\nactive", 8.1),
]
colors = ["#bbbbbb", "#4C72B0", "#4C72B0", "#DD8452", "#55A868"]
for (lab, sub, x), c in zip(stages, colors):
    box = FancyBboxPatch((x-0.65,1.15), 1.3, 0.8, boxstyle="round,pad=0.05",
                         fc=c, ec="black", alpha=0.85)
    axA.add_patch(box)
    axA.text(x, 1.55, lab, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=14, color='white', weight='bold')
    axA.text(x, 0.75, sub, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=9)
# arrows
for x0,x1 in [(1.35,1.75),(3.05,3.45),(4.75,5.25),(6.55,7.45)]:
    axA.add_patch(FancyArrowPatch((x0,1.55),(x1,1.55),arrowstyle='-|>',mutation_scale=18,lw=2,color='black'))
axA.text(2.4,2.35,"1st committed step\n(N-terminal domain)", ha='center', fontsize=8, color='#4C72B0')
axA.text(5.0,2.35,"$\\beta$ before $\\beta'$\n(canonical)", ha='center', fontsize=8, color='#DD8452')
axA.text(7.0,2.35,"$\\omega$ latches $\\beta'$\n(stability)", ha='center', fontsize=8, color='#55A868')
axA.text(6.9,0.25,"noncanonical branch (B. subtilis): $\\alpha_2$ + $\\beta$ OR $\\beta'$ first",
         ha='center', fontsize=8, style='italic', color='grey')
axA.text(9.4,1.55,"+$\\sigma$ →\nholoenzyme\n(OUT of scope)", ha='center', va='center',
         fontsize=8, color='firebrick')
axA.add_patch(FancyArrowPatch((8.75,1.55),(9.0,1.55),arrowstyle='-|>',mutation_scale=14,lw=1.5,color='firebrick',ls='--'))

# ---------- Panel B: catalytic aspartate motif conservation ----------
axB = fig.add_axes([0.05, 0.06, 0.55, 0.4]); axB.axis('off')
axB.set_title("B  Absolutely conserved $\\beta'$ catalytic aspartate motif",
              fontsize=12, loc='left', weight='bold')
# Well-established beta-prime active-site motif region (NADFDGD; the three Asp chelate Mg2+)
species = ["E. coli", "T. aquaticus", "B. subtilis", "M. tuberculosis", "Consensus"]
motif   = ["NADFDGD", "NADFDGD", "NADFDGD", "NADFDGD", "NADFDGD"]
axB.set_xlim(0,7.5); axB.set_ylim(-0.5, len(species))
for i,(sp,mo) in enumerate(zip(species[::-1], motif[::-1])):
    y = i
    axB.text(-0.1, y+0.5, sp, ha='right', va='center', fontsize=10,
             weight=('bold' if sp=='Consensus' else 'normal'))
    for j,aa in enumerate(mo):
        asp = aa=='D'
        fc = '#c0392b' if asp else '#ecf0f1'
        axB.add_patch(mpatches.Rectangle((j,y),0.9,0.9, fc=fc, ec='black', lw=0.6))
        axB.text(j+0.45, y+0.45, aa, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=11,
                 color=('white' if asp else 'black'), weight='bold')
axB.text(3.4, len(species)+0.0, "three invariant Asp (red) chelate the catalytic $Mg^{2+}$",
         ha='center', fontsize=8, color='#c0392b')

# ---------- Panel C: conserved core vs variable periphery ----------
axC = fig.add_axes([0.64, 0.06, 0.32, 0.4]); axC.axis('off')
axC.set_title("C  Conserved core,\nvariable periphery", fontsize=11, loc='left', weight='bold')
axC.set_xlim(0,10); axC.set_ylim(0,10)
# conserved core (inner)
axC.add_patch(mpatches.Circle((5,5),2.4, fc="#55A868", ec='black', alpha=0.9))
axC.text(5,5,"DPBB\ncatalytic\ncore\n(universal)", ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8, color='white', weight='bold')
# variable shell
axC.add_patch(mpatches.Circle((5,5),4.3, fc="none", ec="#DD8452", lw=2, ls='--'))
axC.text(5,9.4,"lineage-specific insertion domains", ha='center', fontsize=7.5, color="#DD8452")
axC.text(5,0.5,"split $\\beta'$: rpoC1/rpoC2\n(cyanobacteria, plastids)", ha='center', fontsize=7.5, color='grey')

plt.savefig("rnap_core_overview.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
print("Figure saved: rnap_core_overview.png")
print("Panel B note: NADFDGD is the canonical, absolutely conserved beta-prime active-site")
print("aspartate motif; the three Asp residues coordinate the catalytic Mg2+ (Zhang et al. 1999).")
